HOME | WORK | POETRY | COMMONPLACE | ABOUT | CONTACT
Featured

Appointed San Francisco's Youth Poet Laureate: A Year of Verse in Service of the City

As SF Youth Poet Laureate 2024, I served as the city's literary ambassador — reading at civic events, leading school workshops, and writing poems that hold San Francisco's stories. The role brought together every strand of my work: language as technology, community as practice, art as civic act.

Read More →
"A city is only as alive as the words its people use to name it."
NONPROFIT

Play-Based Therapy Kits for Children Who Have Experienced Trauma

PlayKits is a nonprofit providing play-based therapy kits to children who have undergone trauma — because healing and play are not separate things.

RESEARCH

Adaptive Learning Interfaces at the Intersection of Neuroscience and Technology

Research at UCSF's Neuroscape lab focuses on building neurofeedback tools that adapt in real-time to a learner's cognitive state.

Latest Work

PlayKits — Play-based therapy kits in action

NONPROFIT

Play-Based Therapy Kits for Children Who Have Experienced Trauma

PlayKits is a nonprofit providing play-based therapy kits to children who have undergone trauma — because healing and play are not separate things.

Neuroscape — Adaptive interface prototype demo

RESEARCH

Adaptive Learning Interfaces at the Intersection of Neuroscience and Technology

Research at UCSF's Neuroscape lab focuses on building neurofeedback tools that adapt in real-time to a learner's cognitive state.

Cardinal Robotics — Facilities staff training program

ENGINEERING

Teaching Facilities Workers to Use Robots — Deployed at Apple, Sutter Health, Cadence, and Whole Foods

As Tech Lead at Cardinal Robotics, I train janitorial and facilities staff to work alongside autonomous cleaning robots — making their work more efficient without replacing their expertise.

"I have learned to read my city by its absences — the laundromat that became a juice bar, the corner store where Mr. Kim's son used to let me run a tab in summer."

— Cartography of Belonging, 2024
The Poetry Desk

Language as Technology, Silence as Signal

Five years of poems exploring displacement, belonging, attention, and what it means to grow up between cultures in a city that is always becoming something else.

Appointed SF Youth Poet Laureate by Mayor Daniel Lurie. Featured on KQED, FOX News California, and the Super Bowl press conference. In partnership with Youth Speaks and 826 Valencia.

Enter the Poetry Archive →

Recognition

SF Youth Poet Laureate 2024 · Davidson Fellows Finalist · UCSF Neuroscape Research Fellowship · PlayKits Community Impact Award · Youth Speaks Grand Slam Finalist
The Commonplace Book

A Constellation of Ideas

A living archive of quotes, annotations, and connections — the intellectual compost pile that feeds everything else.

"The test of a first-rate intelligence is the ability to hold two opposed ideas in the mind at the same time."

— F. Scott Fitzgerald

"Play is the highest form of research."

— Albert Einstein

"Attention is the rarest and purest form of generosity."

— Simone Weil
